summary: Synthesis-boundary review accepts this candidate as a blocked no-op repair. The proposed records preserve the recent systematic-review/meta-analysis gap, record that no source-snapshot-backed review source was screened, and do not add review-derived findings, synthesis groups, or poolability claims.

findings.claim_or_issue: The candidate updates coverage state and durable search/screening trail records but does not claim that the senolytics review-landscape gap has been closed.
findings.why_it_matters: A no-op search repair should improve auditability without converting an unavailable PubMed search into review evidence or synthesis support.
findings.recommended_action: Accept this lane while keeping the review-landscape gap open until a network-enabled search/screening pass creates source-snapshot-backed review records.

findings.claim_or_issue: The proposed records do not add or update synthesis groups, findings, eligibility decisions, source records, or extracted review conclusions.
findings.why_it_matters: This prevents a failed review-landscape search from being mistaken for evidence synthesis or coverage closure.
findings.recommended_action: No revision required; preserve the blocked coverage effect and next-action language in the search log, screening run, research session, and coverage assessment.
